# FilmCan Website Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Build a single static landing page for FilmCan on GitHub Pages — how-it-works, download, Q&A, contact, donate — in the in-app Kodak theme, with the DMG download auto-wired to each release.

**Architecture:** Hand-written static HTML + CSS + vanilla JS in a new `website/` folder. `main.js` fetches `version.json` (generated by the release script) to populate the download links. A GitHub Actions workflow publishes `website/` to GitHub Pages on push to `main`. No framework, no bundler, no build step.

**Tech Stack:** HTML5, CSS3 (custom properties), vanilla ES2020 JS, Google Fonts (Space Grotesk), `sips` for image generation, GitHub Actions (`upload-pages-artifact` + `deploy-pages`).

**Spec:** `docs/specs/2026-06-21-filmcan-website-design.md`

**Conventions for this plan:**
- "Local serve" = `python3 -m http.server 8000 --directory website` (Ctrl-C to stop).
- Static site, no unit-test framework. Verification = `curl` + `grep` on served output, plus a manual browser check at `http://localhost:8000`.
- Canonical links: email `mailto:qtld@pm.me`, repo `https://github.com/qtld88/FilmCan`, releases `https://github.com/qtld88/FilmCan/releases`, Sponsors `https://github.com/sponsors/qtld88`, Ko-fi `https://ko-fi.com/filmcan`.

---

## File Structure

```
website/
  index.html      # single page: nav, hero, how-it-works, features, download, faq, contact/donate, footer
  styles.css      # Kodak theme tokens + all layout
  main.js         # FAQ accordion, smooth scroll, version.json fetch → download links
  version.json    # { version, tag, dmg } — generated by package_release.sh, committed as seed
  assets/
    icon.png
    favicon.png
    apple-touch-icon.png
    og-image.png
    screenshot-main.png
.github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml   # Pages deploy
FilmCan/scripts/package_release.sh   # MODIFY: emit website/version.json
```

---

## Task 1: Scaffold `website/` with assets

**Files:**
- Create: `website/assets/` (via copies below)
- Copy: existing `assets/screenshot-main.png` and `FilmCan/Sources/Resources/Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset/icon_1024x1024.png`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create folder and copy base images**

```bash
mkdir -p website/assets
cp assets/screenshot-main.png website/assets/screenshot-main.png
cp "FilmCan/Sources/Resources/Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset/icon_1024x1024.png" website/assets/icon.png
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Generate favicon, apple-touch-icon, and OG image from the icon**

```bash
sips -z 64 64    website/assets/icon.png --out website/assets/favicon.png
sips -z 180 180  website/assets/icon.png --out website/assets/apple-touch-icon.png
sips -z 630 1200 website/assets/icon.png --out website/assets/og-image.png
```

(`sips -z height width` — OG image is 1200×630; icon is square so it pads/scales acceptably for V1.)

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify assets exist**

Run: `ls -1 website/assets`
Expected: `apple-touch-icon.png  favicon.png  icon.png  og-image.png  screenshot-main.png`

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add website/assets
git commit -m "feat(site): scaffold website assets (icon, favicon, og, hero shot)"
```

---

## Task 2: `styles.css` — theme tokens and full layout

**Files:**
- Create: `website/styles.css`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the stylesheet**

```css
:root{
  --bg:#1B1B1B; --sidebar:#1F1F1F; --panel:#2A2A2A; --card:#2A2A2A;
  --text:#F4F2EE; --yellow:#FFC900; --green:#3B9953; --red:#E45141;
  --stroke:rgba(244,242,238,.18); --stroke2:rgba(244,242,238,.30);
  --maxw:1080px;
}
*{box-sizing:border-box;margin:0;padding:0}
html{scroll-behavior:smooth}
body{background:var(--bg);color:var(--text);font-family:'Space Grotesk',system-ui,-apple-system,sans-serif;line-height:1.5;-webkit-font-smoothing:antialiased}
img{max-width:100%;display:block}
a{color:inherit}
.wrap{max-width:var(--maxw);margin:0 auto;padding:0 28px}
.sub{color:rgba(244,242,238,.65)}
.yellow{color:var(--yellow)}
.btn{background:var(--yellow);color:#1B1B1B;font-weight:600;border:none;border-radius:10px;padding:11px 18px;font-size:14px;text-decoration:none;display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;font-family:inherit;cursor:pointer}
.btn:hover{filter:brightness(1.05)}
.btn.ghost{background:transparent;color:var(--text);border:1px solid var(--stroke2)}
.badge{display:inline-block;background:rgba(59,153,83,.15);color:var(--green);border:1px solid rgba(59,153,83,.4);border-radius:20px;padding:4px 12px;font-size:12px;font-weight:600}
.eyebrow{font-size:12px;letter-spacing:2px;text-transform:uppercase;color:var(--yellow);font-weight:600}
h2.sec{font-size:34px;font-weight:700;margin:8px 0 6px;letter-spacing:-.5px}

/* NAV */
nav{position:sticky;top:0;z-index:10;background:rgba(27,27,27,.85);backdrop-filter:blur(12px);border-bottom:1px solid var(--stroke)}
nav .wrap{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:24px;height:64px}
.brand{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:10px;font-weight:700;font-size:20px}
.brand img{width:30px;height:30px;border-radius:8px}
nav .links{margin-left:auto;display:flex;gap:22px;align-items:center}
nav .links a{text-decoration:none;font-size:14px;font-weight:500;opacity:.8}
nav .links a:hover{opacity:1}

/* HERO */
.hero{padding:80px 0 56px;text-align:center}
.hero h1{font-size:54px;font-weight:700;letter-spacing:-1px;line-height:1.05;margin-top:18px}
.hero p.lead{font-size:20px;max-width:620px;margin:20px auto 30px;color:rgba(244,242,238,.7)}
.hero .cta{display:flex;gap:14px;justify-content:center;margin-bottom:14px}
.meta{font-size:13px;color:rgba(244,242,238,.5)}
.shot{margin:46px auto 0;max-width:880px;border-radius:16px;border:1px solid var(--stroke2);box-shadow:0 30px 80px rgba(0,0,0,.5)}

/* SECTIONS */
section{padding:64px 0;border-top:1px solid var(--stroke)}
.steps{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr);gap:16px;margin-top:36px}
.step{background:var(--card);border:1px solid var(--stroke);border-radius:14px;padding:22px}
.step .n{width:30px;height:30px;border-radius:8px;background:var(--yellow);color:#1B1B1B;font-weight:700;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;margin-bottom:14px}
.step h3{font-size:17px;margin-bottom:6px}
.step p{font-size:14px;color:rgba(244,242,238,.65)}
.feat{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr);gap:16px;margin-top:36px}
.fc{background:var(--card);border:1px solid var(--stroke);border-radius:14px;padding:22px}
.fc .ic{font-size:22px;margin-bottom:10px}
.fc h3{font-size:16px;margin-bottom:6px}
.fc p{font-size:13.5px;color:rgba(244,242,238,.65)}

/* DOWNLOAD */
.dl{background:linear-gradient(135deg,#2A2A2A,#222);border:1px solid var(--stroke2);border-radius:18px;padding:44px;text-align:center}
.dl h2{font-size:30px;font-weight:700;margin-bottom:8px}
.dl .cta{display:flex;gap:14px;justify-content:center;margin:22px 0 10px}

/* FAQ */
.faq{margin-top:30px;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:10px}
.qa{background:var(--card);border:1px solid var(--stroke);border-radius:12px;overflow:hidden}
.qa button{all:unset;cursor:pointer;display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:center;width:100%;padding:18px 20px;font-size:15px;font-weight:600;font-family:inherit}
.qa button .plus{color:var(--yellow);font-size:20px;transition:transform .2s}
.qa[open] button .plus{transform:rotate(45deg)}
.qa .ans{max-height:0;overflow:hidden;transition:max-height .25s ease;padding:0 20px}
.qa[open] .ans{max-height:240px;padding:0 20px 18px}
.qa .ans p{font-size:14px;color:rgba(244,242,238,.65)}

/* CONTACT / DONATE */
.cols{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:16px;margin-top:30px}
.box{background:var(--card);border:1px solid var(--stroke);border-radius:14px;padding:26px}
.box h3{font-size:18px;margin-bottom:10px}
.box a.line{text-decoration:none;display:block;padding:10px 0;border-bottom:1px solid var(--stroke);font-size:14px}
.box a.line:last-child{border-bottom:none}
.box a.line:hover{color:var(--yellow)}

/* FOOTER */
footer{border-top:1px solid var(--stroke);padding:34px 0}
footer .wrap{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:center;font-size:13px;color:rgba(244,242,238,.5)}
footer a{text-decoration:none;color:rgba(244,242,238,.5)}
footer a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}

/* RESPONSIVE */
@media(max-width:820px){
  .steps{grid-template-columns:repeat(2,1fr)}
  .feat{grid-template-columns:repeat(2,1fr)}
}
@media(max-width:640px){
  .hero h1{font-size:38px}
  .hero p.lead{font-size:17px}
  nav .links a:not(.btn){display:none}
  .steps,.feat,.cols{grid-template-columns:1fr}
  footer .wrap{flex-direction:column;gap:10px}
}

/* MOTION */
@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){
  html{scroll-behavior:auto}
  *{transition:none!important}
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit**

```bash
git add website/styles.css
git commit -m "feat(site): add Kodak-theme stylesheet"
```

## Task 3: `index.html` — full page markup

**Files:**
- Create: `website/index.html`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the page**

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
<title>FilmCan — verified camera-card backup for macOS</title>
<meta name="description" content="FilmCan copies your camera media to multiple drives in one pass, hash-verifies every byte, and writes the manifests — so you know the backup is good before you wipe the card.">
<link rel="icon" href="assets/favicon.png">
<link rel="apple-touch-icon" href="assets/apple-touch-icon.png">
<meta property="og:title" content="FilmCan — verified camera-card backup for macOS">
<meta property="og:description" content="Backup camera cards. Verified. To every drive at once.">
<meta property="og:image" content="assets/og-image.png">
<meta property="og:type" content="website">
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Space+Grotesk:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
<link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css">
</head>
<body>

<nav><div class="wrap">
  <a class="brand" href="#" style="text-decoration:none"><img src="assets/icon.png" alt="FilmCan icon">FilmCan</a>
  <div class="links">
    <a href="#how">How it works</a>
    <a href="#features">Features</a>
    <a href="#faq">FAQ</a>
    <a href="#support">Support</a>
    <!-- Docs nav slot reserved for phase 2: <a href="docs/">Docs</a> -->
    <a class="btn" href="#download">Download</a>
  </div>
</div></nav>

<header class="hero"><div class="wrap">
  <span class="badge">Free · GPL-3.0 · macOS 13+</span>
  <h1>Backup camera cards.<br><span class="yellow">Verified.</span> To every drive at once.</h1>
  <p class="lead">FilmCan copies your media to multiple drives in a single pass, hash-verifies every byte, and writes the manifests — so you know the backup is good before you wipe the card.</p>
  <div class="cta">
    <a class="btn" id="dl-hero" href="https://github.com/qtld88/FilmCan/releases">⬇ Download for macOS</a>
    <a class="btn ghost" href="https://github.com/qtld88/FilmCan">View on GitHub</a>
  </div>
  <div class="meta" id="meta-hero">Universal · macOS 13 Ventura or later</div>
  <img class="shot" src="assets/screenshot-main.png" alt="FilmCan app showing a backup copying to two drives, hash-verified.">
</div></header>

<section id="how"><div class="wrap">
  <span class="eyebrow">How it works</span>
  <h2 class="sec">Four steps to a safe backup</h2>
  <div class="steps">
    <div class="step"><div class="n">1</div><h3>Add sources</h3><p>Drag your card or folder into Copy From.</p></div>
    <div class="step"><div class="n">2</div><h3>Add destinations</h3><p>Drop one or more drives into Save To. More drives = redundancy.</p></div>
    <div class="step"><div class="n">3</div><h3>Run</h3><p>One pass copies to every destination at once.</p></div>
    <div class="step"><div class="n">4</div><h3>Verify</h3><p>Hash-checks every byte and writes the manifest.</p></div>
  </div>
</div></section>

<section id="features"><div class="wrap">
  <span class="eyebrow">Features</span>
  <h2 class="sec">Built for the DIT cart</h2>
  <div class="feat">
    <div class="fc"><div class="ic">🗂️</div><h3>Fan-out copy</h3><p>N sources → M drives, read once, written everywhere in a single pass.</p></div>
    <div class="fc"><div class="ic">✅</div><h3>xxHash128 verify</h3><p>Fast or Paranoid re-read. ASC MHL plus chain manifests.</p></div>
    <div class="fc"><div class="ic">⏯️</div><h3>Stop &amp; resume</h3><p>Clean stop, no partial files. Re-run skips what's already safe.</p></div>
    <div class="fc"><div class="ic">🎬</div><h3>Netflix Ingest</h3><p>Camera/Sound routing, roll folders, report scaffolds.</p></div>
    <div class="fc"><div class="ic">📁</div><h3>Organization presets</h3><p>Auto folder structure by date, episode, unit, and format.</p></div>
    <div class="fc"><div class="ic">🔒</div><h3>Local &amp; private</h3><p>No file uploads. Transfers stay on your machine.</p></div>
  </div>
</div></section>

<section id="download"><div class="wrap">
  <div class="dl">
    <h2>Download FilmCan</h2>
    <p class="sub">Free and open source. Universal build for Apple Silicon &amp; Intel.</p>
    <div class="cta">
      <a class="btn" id="dl-main" href="https://github.com/qtld88/FilmCan/releases">⬇ Download (.dmg)</a>
      <a class="btn ghost" href="https://github.com/qtld88/FilmCan/releases">All releases / older versions</a>
    </div>
    <div class="meta" id="meta-download">Requires macOS 13 Ventura or later</div>
  </div>
</div></section>

<section id="faq"><div class="wrap">
  <span class="eyebrow">Q&amp;A</span>
  <h2 class="sec">Frequently asked</h2>
  <div class="faq">
    <details class="qa"><summary><button type="button">Is it free?<span class="plus">+</span></button></summary><div class="ans"><p>Yes. GPL-3.0 licensed and fully open source.</p></div></details>
    <details class="qa"><summary><button type="button">Does it work with my camera?<span class="plus">+</span></button></summary><div class="ans"><p>Any camera that mounts as storage — RED, ARRI, Sony, Canon, Panasonic, Blackmagic, GoPro, and more.</p></div></details>
    <details class="qa"><summary><button type="button">Does it upload anything?<span class="plus">+</span></button></summary><div class="ans"><p>No file uploads. Transfers stay local; optional notifications send status metadata only.</p></div></details>
    <details class="qa"><summary><button type="button">Can I stop and resume?<span class="plus">+</span></button></summary><div class="ans"><p>Yes. Stop is clean with no partial files, and re-running skips files already backed up to every destination.</p></div></details>
    <details class="qa"><summary><button type="button">How does verification work?<span class="plus">+</span></button></summary><div class="ans"><p>Fast checks the hash computed during copy; Paranoid re-reads every file from disk. FilmCan uses xxHash128.</p></div></details>
    <details class="qa"><summary><button type="button">Which macOS is supported?<span class="plus">+</span></button></summary><div class="ans"><p>macOS 13.0 (Ventura) and later.</p></div></details>
  </div>
</div></section>

<section id="support"><div class="wrap">
  <span class="eyebrow">Get in touch · Support the project</span>
  <h2 class="sec">Contact &amp; donate</h2>
  <div class="cols">
    <div class="box">
      <h3>Contact</h3>
      <a class="line" href="mailto:qtld@pm.me">✉ qtld@pm.me</a>
      <a class="line" href="https://github.com/qtld88/FilmCan">🐙 github.com/qtld88/FilmCan</a>
      <a class="line" href="https://github.com/qtld88/FilmCan/issues">🐞 Report a bug / request a feature</a>
    </div>
    <div class="box">
      <h3>Donate</h3>
      <p class="sub" style="font-size:14px;margin-bottom:8px">FilmCan is free. Support keeps it going.</p>
      <a class="line" href="https://github.com/sponsors/qtld88">❤ GitHub Sponsors</a>
      <a class="line" href="https://ko-fi.com/filmcan">☕ Ko-fi · ko-fi.com/filmcan</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</div></section>

<footer><div class="wrap">
  <div>© 2026 FilmCan · GPL-3.0</div>
  <div>
    <a href="https://github.com/qtld88/FilmCan">GitHub</a> ·
    <a href="mailto:qtld@pm.me">Email</a>
  </div>
</div></footer>

<script src="main.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Serve and verify key elements are present**

Run:
```bash
python3 -m http.server 8000 --directory website >/dev/null 2>&1 &
SRV=$!; sleep 1
curl -s http://localhost:8000/ | grep -c 'id="dl-main"'
curl -s http://localhost:8000/ | grep -o 'mailto:qtld@pm.me' | head -1
curl -s http://localhost:8000/ | grep -o 'github.com/sponsors/qtld88' | head -1
curl -s http://localhost:8000/ | grep -o 'ko-fi.com/filmcan' | head -1
kill $SRV
```
Expected: `1`, then `mailto:qtld@pm.me`, `github.com/sponsors/qtld88`, `ko-fi.com/filmcan`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Manual browser check**

Open `http://localhost:8000` (serve as above). Confirm: dark theme renders, hero screenshot loads, all 6 sections present, nav links scroll to sections.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add website/index.html
git commit -m "feat(site): add landing page markup"
```

## Task 4: `main.js` — FAQ accordion + version-driven download links

**Files:**
- Create: `website/main.js`
- Create: `website/version.json`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the seed `version.json`**

```json
{
  "version": "1.3.1",
  "tag": "Release_1.3.1",
  "dmg": "FilmCan-1.3.1-universal.dmg"
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Write `main.js`**

```javascript
// Single-open FAQ accordion using <details>
document.querySelectorAll('.qa').forEach((d) => {
  d.addEventListener('toggle', () => {
    if (d.open) {
      document.querySelectorAll('.qa[open]').forEach((o) => { if (o !== d) o.open = false; });
    }
  });
});

// Populate download links from version.json
const REPO = 'https://github.com/qtld88/FilmCan';
fetch('version.json')
  .then((r) => (r.ok ? r.json() : Promise.reject()))
  .then(({ version, tag, dmg }) => {
    const dmgUrl = `${REPO}/releases/download/${tag}/${dmg}`;
    const main = document.getElementById('dl-main');
    const hero = document.getElementById('dl-hero');
    if (main) { main.href = dmgUrl; main.textContent = `⬇ Download ${version} (.dmg)`; }
    if (hero) { hero.href = dmgUrl; }
    const md = document.getElementById('meta-download');
    const mh = document.getElementById('meta-hero');
    if (md) md.textContent = `FilmCan ${version} · universal · requires macOS 13 Ventura or later`;
    if (mh) mh.textContent = `FilmCan ${version} · universal · macOS 13 Ventura or later`;
  })
  .catch(() => { /* fallback: buttons already point at /releases */ });
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Serve and verify the version wiring**

Run:
```bash
python3 -m http.server 8000 --directory website >/dev/null 2>&1 &
SRV=$!; sleep 1
curl -s http://localhost:8000/version.json | grep -o '"version": "1.3.1"'
curl -s http://localhost:8000/main.js | grep -o 'releases/download/'
kill $SRV
```
Expected: `"version": "1.3.1"` and `releases/download/`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Manual browser check**

Open `http://localhost:8000`. Confirm: download buttons now read "Download 1.3.1 (.dmg)" and link to `…/releases/download/Release_1.3.1/FilmCan-1.3.1-universal.dmg`; clicking a FAQ row expands it and opening another closes the first.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add website/main.js website/version.json
git commit -m "feat(site): faq accordion + version-driven download links"
```

---

## Task 5: Wire `version.json` generation into the release script

**Files:**
- Modify: `FilmCan/scripts/package_release.sh`

- [ ] **Step 1: Inspect how the script derives version/tag/DMG name**

Run: `grep -nE 'VERSION|TAG|\.dmg|dmg|universal' FilmCan/scripts/package_release.sh`
Expected: shows the variables holding the version string and the produced DMG filename. Note their exact names (e.g. `$VERSION`, `$DMG_NAME`). Use those in Step 2 — do not invent names.

- [ ] **Step 2: Append a step that writes `website/version.json`**

Add near the end of the script, after the DMG is built, using the script's actual variable names discovered in Step 1. Template (replace `$VERSION` / `$DMG_NAME` with the real ones; `REPO_ROOT` should resolve to the repo root the script already computes, or `$(git -C "$(dirname "$0")" rev-parse --show-toplevel)`):

```bash
# --- Update website download metadata ---
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
TAG="Release_${VERSION}"
cat > "${REPO_ROOT}/website/version.json" <<EOF
{
  "version": "${VERSION}",
  "tag": "${TAG}",
  "dmg": "${DMG_NAME}"
}
EOF
echo "Wrote website/version.json for ${VERSION}"
```

If the existing release tags do not follow `Release_<version>` exactly, set `TAG` to match the script's real tag convention instead.

- [ ] **Step 3: Dry-run the JSON block in isolation**

Run:
```bash
VERSION=9.9.9 DMG_NAME=FilmCan-9.9.9-universal.dmg REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" \
bash -c 'TAG="Release_${VERSION}"; cat > /tmp/vj.json <<EOF
{
  "version": "${VERSION}",
  "tag": "${TAG}",
  "dmg": "${DMG_NAME}"
}
EOF'; cat /tmp/vj.json
```
Expected: valid JSON with version `9.9.9`, tag `Release_9.9.9`, dmg `FilmCan-9.9.9-universal.dmg`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Restore the real seed version.json (the dry run did not touch it; confirm)**

Run: `cat website/version.json`
Expected: still shows `1.3.1`. If changed, restore: `git checkout website/version.json`.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add FilmCan/scripts/package_release.sh
git commit -m "build(site): emit website/version.json on release"
```

---

## Task 6: GitHub Actions Pages deploy workflow

**Files:**
- Create: `.github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the workflow**

```yaml
name: Deploy website to Pages

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths: ['website/**', '.github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml']
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  pages: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: pages
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: github-pages
      url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/configure-pages@v5
      - uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
        with:
          path: website
      - id: deployment
        uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Validate YAML syntax**

Run: `python3 -c "import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml')); print('valid')"`
Expected: `valid`
(If PyYAML is missing: `python3 -m pip install --user pyyaml` then re-run.)

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add .github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml
git commit -m "ci(site): deploy website/ to GitHub Pages on push to main"
```

- [ ] **Step 4: One-time manual setting (note for the user — not an automated step)**

In the GitHub repo: **Settings → Pages → Build and deployment → Source = GitHub Actions.** Required once before the workflow can publish. Custom domain (optional) configured on the same page.

---

## Task 7: Final integration verification

**Files:** none (verification only)

- [ ] **Step 1: Full local smoke test**

Run:
```bash
python3 -m http.server 8000 --directory website >/dev/null 2>&1 &
SRV=$!; sleep 1
echo "index:";      curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" http://localhost:8000/
echo "styles:";     curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" http://localhost:8000/styles.css
echo "main.js:";    curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" http://localhost:8000/main.js
echo "version:";    curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" http://localhost:8000/version.json
echo "hero img:";   curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" http://localhost:8000/assets/screenshot-main.png
echo "favicon:";    curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" http://localhost:8000/assets/favicon.png
kill $SRV
```
Expected: every line prints `200`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Manual responsive check**

Open `http://localhost:8000`, resize the window narrow (<640px): steps/features/contact collapse to one column, nav text links hide (Download button stays), footer stacks. Wide: 4-up steps, 3-up features.

- [ ] **Step 3: Manual link audit**

Click each: Download (→ release DMG), View on GitHub, All releases, email, Sponsors, Ko-fi, bug report. Each opens the correct canonical URL.

- [ ] **Step 4: Confirm clean tree**

Run: `git status --short`
Expected: empty (all work committed).

---

## Self-Review Notes

- **Spec coverage:** hosting/Pages (Task 6), `website/` layout (Tasks 1–4), theme tokens (Task 2), all 8 page sections incl. reserved Docs slot (Task 3), download wiring + version.json (Tasks 4–5), release-script automation (Task 5), canonical links (Task 3), generated assets (Task 1), responsive + a11y + reduced-motion (Tasks 2, 7). Phase-2 wiki intentionally not built — reserved nav slot only.
- **Naming consistency:** element ids `dl-main`/`dl-hero`/`meta-download`/`meta-hero` and `version.json` keys `version`/`tag`/`dmg` are identical across index.html, main.js, version.json, and package_release.sh.
- **No placeholders:** every code step has complete content; Task 5 explicitly defers variable names to discovery in Step 1 rather than guessing.
```
